text:
as part of your plan, setup an epic gh issue (or do we already have one? maybe send a sonnet agent to give you an idea of what's on gh issues rn in the background) to track all the work that needs to happen -- I'd like chirp to proove this diagnostic with a tui or repl or some type of cli so that agents can iterate/debug [...] - **Phase B ‚Äî nmp-devtools stream**: ordered open/replace/refresh/close events with owner/provenance/revision/refcount/teardown data; join Trellis `ResourcePlan` ‚Üí `ApplyDependentInterestDelta` outcomes into bounded retained records (today they're test-only traces); correlate with the existing relay diagnostics; and define the **NMP-owned receipt shape** so Chirp never sees `ResourcePlan`/`ResourceKey`/`Graph`.
- **Phase C ‚Äî agent-first prover (your CLI/REPL/TUI ask)**: the MCP server (`why_subscription_open`, `why_view_stale`, `what_closed_this_relay`, `list_scope_inventory`, `replay_session`) plus a headless CLI that drives a real Chirp session against real relays and also loads/scrubs exported capsules. Exit criterion baked into the epic: *an agent can answer "why is my feed empty?" from receipts alone.* This lands **before** the pane, so agents can iterate on the diagnostic itself.
- **Phase D ‚Äî Chirp X-Ray pane** (chirp#30 v1): dev-build-only declared projection/devtools channel; Chirp renders, never parses. [...] **13. [Consider ‚Äî Phase C] `replay_session(trace)` is in the Phase C MCP list but capsules don't exist until Phase E.** Either mark it as a Phase C stub excluded from acceptance, or move it to Phase E's deliverables. [...] - **PRs #2860/#2861** ‚Äî `XrayReceipt` + bounded ordered `XrayReceiptStream` + the Trellis‚Üíreceipt adapter. Most of Phase B.
- **PR #2862** ‚Äî `XrayCapsule` envelope (schema versions, producer metadata, redaction mode, **symbolication manifest**), stable shareable pseudonymization, and `XrayProbe` helpers for all five debug queries. That's the Fable-review findings implemented ‚Äî whoever built it worked straight from the updated epic.
